title: Chicken Chettinad

ingredients.title: For Chicken Marinade:
items: 1 kg chicken (bone-in, cut into medium pieces)

ingredients.title: Other Ingredients:
items: 2 tbsp oil (preferably coconut or vegetable oil)
1 cup water (or chicken stock for richer flavor)

instruction.title: Marinate the Chicken
steps: Mix chicken with turmeric, red chili powder, salt, and lemon juice.

instruction.title: Prepare the Chettinad Masala
steps: Dry roast coriander seeds, cumin seeds, fennel seeds, peppercorns, and dry red chilies until aromatic (2–3 minutes).
Add grated coconut and roast for 1–2 minutes until golden.
Let cool slightly, then grind with garlic and ginger into a smooth paste (use little water if necessary).

instruction.title: Cook the Chicken
steps: Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pan. Add curry leaves and sliced onions; sauté until golden brown.
Add the tomato and cook until soft and oil separates.
Add turmeric powder and the prepared Chettinad masala; sauté for 2–3 minutes until fragrant.
Add marinated chicken and mix well to coat with the masala.
Pour in water (or stock), bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20–25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Adjust salt and chili as needed; cook until chicken is tender and sauce thickens to a rich consistency.

instruction.title: Garnish and Serve
steps: Serve hot with steamed rice, idiyappam, or paratha.

chefTips: Freshly roasted spices make a huge difference; avoid using pre-ground powders for authenticity.
Bone-in chicken absorbs flavors better and makes the curry richer.
Use coconut oil for that authentic South Indian aroma, but you can substitute with vegetable oil if unavailable.
Let the curry rest for 10 minutes before serving to deepen the flavors.
